sections.paragraph:
ἀναγωγεύς, έως, ὁ (on the form ἀγωγεύς s. DELG s.v. ἄγω 18) (lit. ‘that which brings up’, so of straps for holding a sandal in place Ael., VH 9, 11; Athen. 543f) one who leads upward (Proclus, on Pla., Tim. I p. 34, 20 Diehl, of Hermes; Hymni 1, 34 [Orphica p. 277 Abel], of Helios ψυχῶν ἀναγωγεύς), only fig. ἡ πίστις ὑμῶν ἀ. ὑμῶν IEph 9:1 (s. Hdb. ad loc.) the ‘windlass’ of Lghtf. et al. seems unlikely.—TW.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Abel Abel = FA., Grammaire du Grec biblique—List 6

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 62
